chapter: ENG9_8 – A House Is Not a Home
title: A House Is Not a Home – Hard VSAQs

questions.question: What psychological challenge did the author face in his new school?
questions.answer: He struggled with loneliness and insecurity.

questions.question: Why did the author feel isolated despite being in a new school?
questions.answer: Because he had no friends there.

questions.question: What role did the author’s old teachers play in helping him?
questions.answer: They encouraged him to participate in activities and make friends.

questions.question: Why did the author’s mother value the photographs and letters?
questions.answer: They were memories of the author’s late father.

questions.question: What does the fire symbolise in the story?
questions.answer: Sudden loss and life’s unexpected challenges.

questions.question: Why did the author feel helpless watching his house burn?
questions.answer: Because he could do nothing to stop the fire.

questions.question: What emotional loss hurt the author the most?
questions.answer: The loss of his cat.

questions.question: Why did the author describe himself as a zombie at school?
questions.answer: Because he felt emotionally numb.

questions.question: What social fear did the author experience at school?
questions.answer: Fear of being judged by others.

questions.question: Why did the author feel responsible for the attention he received?
questions.answer: Because everyone knew about the fire.

questions.question: What does the classmates’ help symbolise?
questions.answer: Human kindness and empathy.

questions.question: Why was the author surprised by the kindness of his classmates?
questions.answer: Because many of them had never spoken to him before.

questions.question: How did the author’s attitude toward people change?
questions.answer: He realised people could be kind and supportive.

questions.question: What does rebuilding the house symbolise?
questions.answer: Rebuilding of the author’s life.

questions.question: Why did the author say his life was also rebuilding?
questions.answer: Because he gained new friends and confidence.

questions.question: What does the cat represent emotionally in the story?
questions.answer: Comfort and companionship.

questions.question: Why did the woman who found the cat try to locate its owner?
questions.answer: Because she believed it was loved and missed.

questions.question: What lesson does the author learn from the fire?
questions.answer: That kindness and support matter more than possessions.

questions.question: Why does the author feel gratitude at the end?
questions.answer: Because he regained his cat and found new friends.

questions.question: What emotional change happens to the author after the event?
questions.answer: He becomes more open and hopeful.

questions.question: What message does the story convey about community?
questions.answer: Community support helps people recover from tragedy.

questions.question: Why is the title ‘A House Is Not a Home’ meaningful?
questions.answer: Because a home is built with love and relationships.

questions.question: What emotional state did the author move from and to?
questions.answer: From loneliness to belonging.

questions.question: How does the story show the importance of empathy?
questions.answer: Through the kindness of classmates and the woman who returned the cat.

questions.question: What is the central idea of the story?
questions.answer: Human kindness and relationships make life meaningful.
